skill.name: ace-step
skill.description: Generate, inpaint, and outpaint music with ACE Step on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. ACE Step is StepFun-AI's open-weights music foundation model — tag-driven composition (genre, mood, instruments), multilingual lyrics with section markers, 5 s to 4 min stereo output, $0.0002–0.0003 per second (≈ 27× cheaper than ElevenLabs Music). Four endpoints: ACE Step text-to-audio (the default), ACE Step 1.5 text-to-audio (50+ language lyrics, refined structured-lyric handling), ACE Step audio-inpaint (regenerate a time range inside an existing track), ACE Step audio-outpaint (extend an existing track before or after). Triggers on "ace step", "ace-step", "acestep", "ACE music", "open music model", "cheap AI music", "inpaint audio", "audio inpaint", "extend music", "audio outpaint", "lengthen track", "music with tags", or any explicit ask to generate or edit music with ACE Step.
skill.summary: Generate, inpaint, and outpaint music with the ACE Step open-weights model on RunComfy — four CLI endpoints for text-to-audio, multilingual vocals, audio inpainting, and outpainting at ~27× lower cost than competitors.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate, well-documented wrapper for the RunComfy CLI that generates music with the ACE Step model. The static analyzer flagged 146 potential issues, but all are false positives: the 114 'external_commands' detections are Markdown code blocks showing `runcomfy` CLI usage examples (not actual code execution), the 21 'network' detections are legitimate documentation URLs to runcomfy.com, and the 'weak crypto' flags trigger on the word 'base' (base model) and 'seed' (reproducibility seed). The skill declares a tightly-scoped `Bash(runcomfy *)` allowed-tools entry, includes a comprehensive Security & Privacy section documenting token storage, outbound endpoint allowlisting, and indirect prompt injection mitigations. No malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Token storage in user config directory
low_findings.description: The skill documents that the runcomfy CLI stores API tokens in `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` with file mode 0600. This is documented behavior of the upstream CLI, not a vulnerability in the skill itself. The skill explicitly recommends using the `RUNCOMFY_TOKEN` environment variable as a more secure alternative in CI/container environments.

content.user_title: Generate Music with ACE Step AI
content.value_statement: Creating music with AI is expensive and limited to text-to-audio. This skill provides four ACE Step endpoints for generation, inpainting, and outpainting at approximately 27 times lower cost than alternatives, with full control over tag-driven composition and multilingual vocals.
actual_capabilities: Generate stereo music (5–240 seconds) from comma-separated tags describing genre, mood, and instruments
Produce multilingual vocal tracks in 50+ languages using the ACE Step 1.5 variant with section markers like [Verse], [Chorus], [Bridge]
Inpaint existing audio files by regenerating a time-bounded segment (start_time/end_time in seconds) without masking
Outpaint existing tracks bidirectionally to add intros, outros, or extended arrangements
Control cost at $0.0002–0.0003 per second of generated audio with reproducible seeds
Route requests to the optimal endpoint based on user intent (generate, repair, or extend)
limitations: Maximum single-call duration is 4 minutes (240 seconds); longer tracks require chained calls
Inpaint window is time-range based only, not mask-based — edits must be time-bounded
Requires a RunComfy account and API token (free tier available but paid usage for production volumes)
Output quality for premium commercial vocal hooks may not match specialized alternatives like ElevenLabs Music

use_cases.title: Cost-sensitive background music library
use_cases.description: Generate dozens of instrumental tracks with varied genre and mood tags for apps, games, or video projects at minimal cost.
use_cases.target_user: Indie game developers and content creators

use_cases.title: Section repair on existing tracks
use_cases.description: Use audio inpainting to replace a weak chorus, swap a bridge, or fix a problematic 20-second section without re-rendering the full track.

use_cases.title: Multilingual music launch
use_cases.description: Produce the same song in 50+ languages by keeping tags consistent and swapping lyrics, ideal for global marketing campaigns.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and international brand managers

prompt_templates.title: Generate a lo-fi instrumental
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 90-second lo-fi hip-hop instrumental. Use these tags: "lo-fi hip-hop, mellow, vinyl crackle, rhodes piano, soft drums, 75 BPM". Set lyrics to "[inst]". Output to ./out.

prompt_templates.title: Create a full vocal song with structure
prompt_templates.prompt:
Generate a 60-second indie pop song with the following lyrics structure:
[Verse]
Your first verse here
[Chorus]
Your chorus hook
[Bridge]
Soft breakdown
[Outro]
Full band fade

Tags: "indie pop, anthemic, electric guitar, driving drums, female vocal, 120 BPM". Use the ACE Step 1.5 endpoint.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user writing a complete song

prompt_templates.title: Inpaint a problem section
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the track at https://my-cdn.example/track.mp3 and regenerate the 20–40 second section. Tags: "indie pop, breakdown, piano only, soft, no drums". Use start_time: 20, end_time: 40, lyrics: "[inst]".
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user repairing an existing track

prompt_templates.title: Extend a hook into a full track
prompt_templates.prompt: Take this 30-second hook at https://my-cdn.example/hook.mp3 and extend it: add 30 seconds of intro before and 60 seconds of outro after. Tags: "indie pop, electric guitar, drums, build-up before chorus, fade outro". Use the audio-outpaint endpoint with extend_before_duration: 30 and extend_after_duration: 60.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user building full arrangements from short clips

output_examples.input: Generate a 60-second indie pop song with female vocals and a memorable chorus about resilience.
output_examples.output: The skill routes to ACE Step 1.5 (text-to-audio) with tags like "indie pop, anthemic, electric guitar, driving drums, female vocal, 120 BPM" and generated lyrics with [Verse]/[Chorus]/[Bridge] structure. Output: a 60-second stereo MP3/WAV file saved to ./out, costing approximately $0.018.

output_examples.input: Fix the weak bridge in the middle of my track between 1:20 and 1:50.
output_examples.output: The skill invokes the audio-inpaint endpoint with start_time: 80, end_time: 110, and matching style tags. Output: a new version of the track with the bridge segment regenerated to match the surrounding arrangement, saved to ./out.

best_practices: Be specific with tags — "lo-fi hip-hop, mellow, vinyl crackle, rhodes piano, soft drums, 75 BPM" produces better results than "chill music"
Use the base ACE Step endpoint for cost-sensitive drafts and iteration, then escalate to ACE Step 1.5 for final multilingual or hero-quality renders
Fix the seed parameter for reproducible outputs when iterating on prompts; use seed: -1 to explore variations
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to inpaint time ranges that are not well-bounded — the endpoint requires explicit start_time and end_time anchors and cannot do free-form edits
Do not request tracks longer than 4 minutes in a single call — chain multiple outpaint calls instead of exceeding the 240-second limit
Do not pass copyrighted lyrics without confirming rights — the skill generates music around whatever lyrics are provided, and copyright compliance is the operator's responsibility

faq.question: What is ACE Step?
faq.answer: ACE Step is StepFun-AI's open-weights music foundation model, available on RunComfy. It generates stereo music from tags and optional lyrics, supports 50+ languages in the 1.5 variant, and can inpaint or outpaint existing audio.

faq.question: How much does it cost?
faq.answer: ACE Step costs $0.0002 per second of audio. ACE Step 1.5 costs $0.0003 per second. A 60-second track is approximately $0.012 to $0.018 — roughly 27 times cheaper than ElevenLabs Music.

faq.question: What is the maximum track length?
faq.answer: A single generation call supports 5 to 240 seconds (4 minutes). For longer tracks, use the audio-outpaint endpoint to extend an existing track, or chain multiple calls together.

faq.question: How do I install this skill?
faq.answer: Run: npx skills add agentspace-so/runcomfy-agent-skills --skill ace-step -g. You also need the runcomfy CLI installed globally (npm i -g @runcomfy/cli) and an account (runcomfy login).

faq.question: What is the difference between inpaint and outpaint?
faq.answer: Inpaint regenerates a time-bounded section inside an existing track (e.g., replace 20–40 seconds with a new bridge). Outpaint extends a track by adding new audio before or after the original (e.g., add an intro or outro).

faq.question: Is my audio data private?
faq.answer: Audio URLs and tags are transmitted to the RunComfy Model API over HTTPS. The skill does not perform any telemetry or callbacks. Source audio URLs should only be from trusted, user-provided locations, as third-party content could influence generation.
